questions.q: India's first general election was conducted over which span of months?
questions.explanation: India's first general election was held from October 1951 to February 1952.

questions.q: Approximately how many voters were eligible in India's first general election?
questions.explanation: Over 170 million voters were eligible in the first general election.

questions.q: At the time it was held, India's first general election was notable for being the:
options: First election ever held in Asia
Largest democratic exercise in the world
First election held only in cities
questions.explanation: It was the largest democratic exercise in the world at that time.

questions.q: For most voters in India's first general election, the act of voting was:
options: A regular routine they had done before
Something they were doing for the first time
questions.explanation: Most of the over 170 million eligible voters were voting for the first time.

questions.q: India's first general election elected members to which house of Parliament?
questions.explanation: The first general election filled the directly elected Lok Sabha, the House of the People.

questions.q: The first general election was made possible by which voting principle granted by the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Universal adult franchise gave every adult citizen the right to vote, enabling mass participation.

questions.q: In which season of the year did the first general election begin in 1951?
questions.explanation: Polling began in October 1951, during the autumn season.

questions.q: The first general election is often described as which kind of milestone for the new Republic?
questions.explanation: It is remembered as a great democratic experiment for the newly independent Republic.

questions.q: The first general election was held after India became a republic in which year?
questions.explanation: India became a republic in 1950, and the first general election followed in 1951-52.

questions.q: Which body was responsible for administering India's first general election?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India administered elections to Parliament, including the first general election.

questions.q: Who served as the first Chief Election Commissioner who organised India's first general election?
questions.explanation: Sukumar Sen, the first Chief Election Commissioner, organised the first general election of 1951-52.

questions.q: The first general election determined the membership of the first Lok Sabha, which then met for the first time in which year?
questions.explanation: The first Lok Sabha, elected in 1951-52, was constituted in 1952.

questions.q: Who became Prime Minister of India following the first general election?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru continued as Prime Minister after the first general election of 1951-52.

questions.q: The first general election extended over several months largely because of which practical factor?
options: The vast size and logistics of the country
questions.explanation: The enormous size of the country and the logistics of reaching all voters required polling over many months.

questions.q: The first general election demonstrated which feature of India's electorate compared with the world at the time?
options: Its limitation to a single party
questions.explanation: With over 170 million eligible voters, it was the largest electorate exercised anywhere in the world then.

questions.q: Voting in the first general election was open to adult citizens regardless of caste, religion, gender, wealth or education because of which principle?
questions.explanation: Universal adult franchise extended the vote to all adult citizens irrespective of such distinctions.

questions.q: The institution that conducted the first general election was established in which month and year?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India was established on 25 January 1950, before the first general election.

questions.q: Because so many first-time voters were unable to read, the first general election made notable use of which aid on the ballot system?
questions.explanation: Party symbols were used so that voters, many of whom could not read, could identify their choices.

questions.q: The first general election filled seats in the Lok Sabha, whose members are chosen by which method?
questions.explanation: Lok Sabha members are directly elected by the people, as in the first general election.

questions.q: The first general election was conducted under the framework of the Constitution that had come into force in which year?
questions.explanation: The Constitution came into force on 26 January 1950, providing the framework for the first general election.

questions.q: The same Chief Election Commissioner who ran the first general election also oversaw which subsequent national poll?
questions.explanation: Sukumar Sen organised both the first general election of 1951-52 and the second in 1957.

questions.q: In which Himalayan region was polling for the first general election held earliest, before the main 1952 phase, owing to winter snowfall?
questions.explanation: Voting in some high Himalayan areas, in what is now Himachal Pradesh, was held in late 1951 ahead of heavy winter snow.

questions.q: The first general election was the first poll held simultaneously to constitute both the Lok Sabha and which other set of bodies?
options: The Rajya Sabha by direct vote
questions.explanation: The 1951-52 exercise elected both the Lok Sabha and the State Legislative Assemblies.

questions.q: Roughly what share of the over 170 million eligible electors in the first general election were estimated to be unable to read or write?
questions.explanation: A large majority of the electorate was unable to read or write, which is why party symbols were essential.

questions.q: The method of voting used in the first general election relied on which physical instrument, later replaced by Electronic Voting Machines?
questions.explanation: Voters used paper ballots and ballot boxes, a system later replaced by Electronic Voting Machines.

questions.q: The first general election established a precedent that India would choose its national legislature through which constitutional mechanism going forward?
options: Nomination by the head of state
Periodic direct elections under universal adult franchise
questions.explanation: It set the precedent of choosing the Lok Sabha through periodic direct elections under universal adult franchise.

questions.q: To manage the scale of the first general election, authorities prepared electoral rolls covering an electorate of what approximate magnitude?
options: Over a hundred and seventy million
questions.explanation: Electoral rolls were prepared for an eligible electorate of over 170 million people.

questions.q: The first general election was conducted while which interim arrangement of governance had just been replaced by the new constitutional order?
options: The Constituent Assembly acting as a provisional legislature
questions.explanation: Before the first elected Lok Sabha, the Constituent Assembly had served as a provisional legislature until the 1951-52 polls.

questions.q: The first general election is regarded as a landmark because it tested universal adult franchise at a scale unprecedented for a country with which characteristic at the time?
questions.explanation: Holding a free vote on such a scale amid widespread poverty and low literacy made it a remarkable democratic experiment.

questions.q: The body that ran the first general election derives its authority to conduct elections to Parliament from which part of the Constitution?
options: The provisions on the Election Commission and elections
The provisions on the armed forces
questions.explanation: The Election Commission's authority to superintend elections to Parliament comes from the Constitution's provisions on elections.
